name: Classic Mandelbrot Set
summary: The quintessential escape-time fractal. Points in the complex plane are tested for escape under repeated squaring. The boundary contains infinite detail.
notes: Rendered with a bailout of 500 and exposure target of 50 using a log1p-mapped shader to reveal structure near the cardioid and period bulbs.

name: Julia Set
summary: A connected Julia set for a parameter inside the Mandelbrot set. Shows dendritic filaments and spiral structure.
notes: Fixed c = -0.75 + 0.156i gives a dendrite-like shape. Distance field shading highlights filament width.

name: Burning Ship
summary: A variant that takes the absolute value of real and imaginary parts before squaring, producing fractal 'smoke' and ship-like shapes.
notes: Default viewport spans nearly the full Burning Ship set. Smoothed distance estimate avoids banding.

name: Tricorn (Mandelbar)
summary: The conjugate of the Mandelbrot iteration. It has a self-similar anti-holomorphic structure with three-fold symmetries.
notes: Standard viewport centered at origin. Exposure shading reveals the central bulb and satellite copies.

name: Multibrot-3
summary: The degree-3 analogue of the Mandelbrot set. The main cardioid becomes a reentrant shape with three-fold symmetry.
notes: Higher-power Mandelbrots have thinner filaments. A bailout of 500 captures enough orbit detail for clean shading.

name: Newton Fractal (z^3 - 1)
summary: Basins of attraction for Newton's method applied to z^3 - 1 = 0. The three basins meet at the Julia set of the method.
notes: Convergence tolerance set to 1e-8. Iteration count maps nicely to exposure; distance field emphasizes basin boundaries.

name: Glynn Fractal
summary: A complex-analytic fractal with fractional exponent 1.5. Produces a tree-like structure with dense branching.
notes: Viewport centered on the main tree. Logarithmic distance shading highlights fine branch tips.

name: Logistic Map
summary: A one-dimensional chaotic map plotted in the (r, x) plane. Classic example of period-doubling route to chaos.
notes: Full bifurcation diagram from r=2 to r=4. High exposure target captures transient chaotic bands.

name: Buddhabrot
formula: z_{n+1} = z_n^2 + c, \quad z_0 = 0; plot escaping orbit points instead of parameter points
summary: A trajectory-density rendering of the Mandelbrot iteration. Instead of coloring c directly, it accumulates the paths of escaping orbits, revealing ghostly basin structure around the Mandelbrot set.
notes: Stochastic CPU render with modest exposure target for the daily run. Lower cutoff suppresses early orbit points while preserving the characteristic nebula-like density field.

name: Coverage-Guided Buddhabrot
formula: z_{n+1} = z_n^2 + c, \quad z_0 = 0; accumulate escaping orbit points until a target percentage of pixels is hit
summary: A Buddhabrot rendering variant that keeps sampling escaping Mandelbrot orbits until a specified image-coverage target is reached, making the stopping condition density-driven rather than exposure-driven.
notes: Daily preset uses a modest 5% coverage target at 512×512 resolution to keep the stochastic CPU render bounded while still revealing the familiar nebula-like orbit-density structure.

name: Magnet Type II Fractal
summary: A higher-degree rational-map magnet fractal. Points in the parameter plane are iterated toward the attracting fixed point z = 1, with poles and basin boundaries producing dense circular filigree.
notes: Full symmetric parameter-plane viewport. The rational denominator creates sharp singular structures, so guarded division and convergence checks are used during CPU rendering.

name: Celtic Mandelbrot
summary: A Mandelbrot-family escape-time fractal that folds the real component of z^2 before adding c, producing Celtic-knot-like lobes and mirrored boundary filaments.
notes: Rendered on the symmetric parameter-plane viewport. The absolute-value fold creates cusped bridges and repeated knot-like motifs distinct from the classic Mandelbrot cardioid.

name: Perpendicular Mandelbrot
summary: A Mandelbrot-family escape-time fractal that folds only the real component of z before squaring. The perpendicular fold changes the symmetry of the quadratic map and produces vertical, ship-like tendrils along the boundary.
notes: Rendered on a symmetric parameter-plane viewport. The single-axis fold is gentler than the Burning Ship's two-axis absolute-value fold, preserving more Mandelbrot-like structure while introducing perpendicular filaments.

name: Multibrot-4
summary: The degree-4 analogue of the Mandelbrot set. Raising each orbit to the fourth power produces a three-armed symmetric parameter-plane set with thinner lobes and sharper branching than the quadratic Mandelbrot.
notes: Rendered on a centered symmetric viewport. The even power creates mirrored lobes and fine boundary filaments; log1p exposure shading keeps both high-iteration interiors and narrow exterior tendrils visible.

name: Perpendicular Burning Ship
summary: A Burning Ship-family escape-time fractal that folds the imaginary component before squaring. The one-axis fold creates ship-like mirrored structures while preserving a different handedness from the real-fold perpendicular Mandelbrot.
notes: Rendered on a symmetric parameter-plane viewport. The imaginary-axis fold produces sharp mirrored wakes and cusped boundary features distinct from both the classic Mandelbrot and the full two-axis Burning Ship.
